search_queries_used: site:diabetesjournals.org 2026 glycemic status assessment hyperglycemic crises follow-up
site:dha.gov.ae type 2 diabetes red flags hyperglycaemia telehealth guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic laboratory testing, fluids, insulin or admission
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Hyperglycaemic emergencies require clinician assessment and measured data.
No investigation, treatment, dose, urgency or disposition is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with clinician-recognised hyperglycaemia; pregnancy, children, type 1 diabetes and acute metabolic decompensation require distinct pathways.
setting_applicability: Outpatient follow-up with emergency concern prompts remaining manual.
UAE_applicability: DHA is UAE-direct for adult type 2 diabetes telehealth; ADA guidance requires local laboratory and emergency-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 and ADA Standards 2026 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current registered official versions were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact glycaemic-status section supports clinician review of existing laboratory or home-monitoring data, interval change and an entered follow-up plan without diagnosing a crisis or applying a target.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of A1C and/or CGM metrics and an individualised assessment frequency without generating a target or interpretation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag section supports manual documentation of severe or worsening symptoms, abnormal measured observations and clinician concern requiring escalation; no emergency conclusion or action is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

unresolved_source_gaps: Symptoms, hydration, ketones, glucose values, diabetes type, pregnancy, medicines, adherence, diagnosis, investigations, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
